commands.summary: observed and same-class Gmail/content-budget and batch-summary hazards pass
large-ledger relation index and object-browser hazards pass
M0/C1-C12 reattachment and closure pass
routine TestMesh pass
model-code-test alignment pass
installed synthetic desktop UI checks pass
UI structure and current runtime evidence pass
G0-G8 current with explicit G9-G12 evidence routes

counterexamples: Connector-supplied Gmail bodies could be starved by opaque metadata-first ordering.
Per-finding stage updates could rewrite the global coverage summary repeatedly.
Per-card coverage lookup could rescan the whole private ledger.
An unbounded UI verifier could survive launcher timeout and leave browser descendants.
findings: C1 now prioritizes authorized supplied bodies within the exact content budget.
Coverage summary refresh occurs once at the terminal batch join.
C12 uses a durable Matter-to-coverage index with transactional maintenance.
Live UI verification now has bounded waits, phase evidence, and guaranteed browser cleanup.
DevelopmentProcessFlow now consumes privacy-minimized G9, current installed G10, package-boundary G11, and frozen-local-release G12 evidence without overclaiming open private coverage.
friction_points: The installed browser verifier initially targeted the validation container instead of its private data root.
The shared FlowGuard upgrade required direct canonical Behavior Commitment Ledger expansion before DPF could resume.
metadata: This entry records public model-miss classes, repairs, synthetic/runtime validation routes, and explicit remaining release boundaries. It contains no private source values, paths, ids, excerpts, hashes, screenshots, or model payloads.
bounded aggregate only; complete semantic coverage remains open
next_actions: Run one frozen M0/C1-C12 owner and one frozen S0-S5 owner.
Run full tests and TM19 in a clean clone.
Build, install, tag, and rerun G12 against exact synchronized identities.
risk_evidence_summary: Gmail authorized-body prioritization is bounded by content_limit.
Large object-browser relation lookup is indexed.
Installed UI evidence is phase-bounded and cleanup-safe.
skipped_steps: Complete private semantic coverage is intentionally not claimed.
Figma evidence, Linux CI, GitHub publication, and license authorization remain external or future gates.
task_summary: Close observed autonomous object-browser model misses and route G9-G12 evidence
trigger_reason: real Gmail canary, large private ledger, installed UI, and release closure exposed post-green misses

commands.summary: all 23 suites executed, exposing incorrect routine-only parent status
release/routine parent-status regression passed

counterexamples: A full 23-suite release run was serialized as routine_green with a claim that TM19 remained deferred.
G8 rejected a current release TestMesh because it accepted only the routine deferred shape.
findings: The parent receipt now emits release_green only when no suite remains deferred.
The release claim boundary names TM01-TM23 and preserves the incomplete-private-coverage boundary.
G8 accepts either the exact routine shape or the exact release shape while still requiring a native-green report.
friction_points: The first clean-clone release run passed its tests but exposed a validator-state mismatch, so its receipt was not accepted as final evidence.
metadata: This entry covers TestMesh parent-status and G8 release-shape validation only; it does not replace a fresh full release run.
release receipt state hardcoded to routine shape
next_actions: Create a new clean clone and run one accepted final TM01-TM23 owner.
Freeze release evidence only after G8 and public boundaries consume the release-green receipt.
risk_evidence_summary: A release-only suite can no longer be both executed and described as deferred.
Routine and release evidence retain distinct statuses and claim boundaries.
skipped_steps: The superseded first clean-clone receipt is not final release evidence.
task_summary: Repair TestMesh release-state and G8 acceptance semantics
trigger_reason: the first full clean-clone release owner passed every suite but the parent validator retained a routine-only status
